title: 谋杀启事
summary:
Colquhoun questions Mrs. Lucas regarding the work habits of Mrs. Hemmings. Mrs. Lucas complains that the widow is frequently absent during work hours and suggests that Mrs. Hemmings should send her children to summer camps, a suggestion that was reportedly rejected. Colquhoun observes that Mrs. Hemmings receives lower wages than most.

In a separate interrogation, Mrs. Sweetnam recounts the robbery to Edmund. She describes how the lights went out and a figure appeared with a revolver, demanding "Money or your life!" She recalls being near the clock and the fireplace when a bullet whizzed past and a flashlight was shone in their eyes. Edmund adds that he was in the long room with Julia Simmons and notes that Philipa Hemmings was by the fireplace on the far side of the room. Edmund also clarifies that the dining room door had been locked from the outside.

Detective Kolar interviews Colonel Eastbrook, who offers a psychological theory regarding the crime. The Colonel argues that the perpetrator placed the advertisement to gain attention and intended to play the role of a "tough guy," but lost control when the situation became real. He suggests the man fired aimlessly, panicked after hitting someone, and then committed suicide. The Colonel and his wife claim they were standing by a table with flowers during the shooting.

Finally, Inspector Corradino questions Miss Chickering in a pig pen. She states she was by the fireplace during the incident and expresses confusion as to why the man did not rob someone on the road instead. She recalls the flashlight blinding the group and mentions that she initially thought Patrick Simmons might have been playing with a loaded revolver.
